content.problemStatement:
Let F(S)F(S)F(S) denote the number of **distinct** elements in the array SSS. For example, F([1,2,3,2])=3,F([1,2])=2.F([1, 2, 3, 2]) = 3, F([1, 2]) = 2.F([1,2,3,2])=3,F([1,2])=2.

You are given an array AAA containing NNN integers. Find if it is possible to divide the elements of the array AAA into two arrays BBB and CCC such that :


Every element of the array AAA belongs either to array BBB or to array CCC.
F(B)=F(C)F(B) = F(C)F(B)=F(C).

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the length of the array AAA.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integer A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, print `YES` if it is possible to divide the elements of the array AAA into two arrays B,CB, CB,C satisfying all the conditions and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One possible division is B=[1],C=[2]B = [1], C = [2]B=[1],C=[2]. Here F(B)=F(C)=1.F(B) = F(C) = 1.F(B)=F(C)=1.
Test case 222: There is no way to distribute the elements of the array A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3] satisfying all the conditions.
Test case 333: One possible division is B=[3,1],C=[1,2]B = [3, 1], C = [1, 2]B=[3,1],C=[1,2]. Here F(B)=F(C)=2.F(B) = F(C) = 2.F(B)=F(C)=2.
